channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: How Iran’s control of the Strait of Hormuz reshapes the Philippines’ oil vulnerability
item.description: The article by Val A. Villanueva analyzes how the ongoing instability in the Middle East—specifically Iran’s grip on the Strait of Hormuz—continues to affect global oil prices and, in turn, the Philip...

item.title: A guide to Montréal’s standout restaurants, cafés, bars, and food experiences
item.description: Montréal’s culinary scene is presented as one of the most distinctive and dynamic in North America, shaped by generations of immigration and a strong mix of cultural influences. The city blends classi...

item.title: Astrological Insights and Guidance for April 14, 2026
item.description: The Globe and Mail’s astrologer Sally Brompton offers a set of horoscopes for April 14, 2026, highlighting positive cosmic influences and new beginnings across the zodiac signs. For those celebrating ...

item.title: Why helium is essential to modern technology and why global shortages are raising concern
item.description: This podcast episode from The Guardian’s Science Weekly explores the surprising importance of helium in modern life and the growing concerns over its global shortage. Helium, often associated with bal...

item.title: Hungary’s vote for change after Viktor Orbán’s defeat
item.description: Hungary’s political landscape has undergone a major shift with Viktor Orbán’s ouster after 16 years in power. The editorial examines how the election result reflects deep public frustration with Orbán...

item.title: Woman receives fine and community order for selling fake Oasis tickets
item.description: A 33-year-old woman from Betley, Newcastle-under-Lyme, Rosie Slater, has been fined £40 and handed a 12-month community order for selling fake tickets to Oasis concerts at Wembley. In May, she scammed...

item.title: Google and Fitbit revive minimalist tracking with new screenless fitness band
item.description: Google is developing a new Fitbit fitness tracker that abandons screens entirely, signaling a return to the minimalist philosophy that defined Fitbit's early success. Revealed through a teaser video f...

item.title: Pakistan introduces national medical tourism initiative to strengthen healthcare and surgical services under SIFC
item.description: The Government of Pakistan has officially launched the Pakistan National Medical Tourism Initiative under the Special Investment Facilitation Council (SIFC), aiming to position the country as a region...

item.title: Father devastated after family dog fatally attacks teenage daughter in Essex
item.description: In Essex, England, a tragic event unfolded when 19-year-old Jamie-Lea Biscoe was mauled to death by her family’s dog, a blue-merle Lurcher-cross named Shy. Her father, Jack Biscoe, discovered the horr...

item.title: Profile of Péter Magyar and the reported Hungarian election outcome
item.description: The article presents Péter Magyar as Hungary’s newly elected prime minister following a major electoral upset against long-serving leader Viktor Orbán. According to the report, Magyar’s rise from rela...

item.title: NHS nurse reaches settlement following suspension over transgender patient pronoun incident
item.description: A nurse from south London has reached a settlement with her NHS employer after a dispute involving the use of pronouns for a transgender patient led to her suspension and a subsequent legal challenge....

item.title: A Reflection on Mortality and Meaning in Tolstoy’s The Death of Ivan Ilyich
item.description: This article explores Leo Tolstoy’s novella ‘The Death of Ivan Ilyich’ and what it reveals about life, death, and the meaning of existence. The writer, Jane Shussa, reflects on how people usually thin...

item.title: Alan Peter Cayetano remains Senate Minority Leader; Tito Sotto still Senate President
item.description: Rappler debunked a viral social media post falsely claiming that Senator Alan Peter Cayetano had replaced Senator Tito Sotto as the new Senate President. The post, published on April 4, 2026, featured...

item.title: California State Bar alleges attorneys used AI-generated filings with fabricated legal citations
item.description: Three California attorneys are facing disciplinary action after allegations that they submitted court filings containing fake or irrelevant legal citations generated with the help of artificial intell...

item.title: Meru Boy Gives Clear Directions Using Local Landmarks While Family Faces Medical Crisis
item.description: Musa Kihara, a young PP1 pupil from Gituja Primary School in Meru, has captured the attention of Kenyans online after a video of him giving directions to his home went viral. Calmly and patiently, Mus...

item.title: US-Iran talks in Islamabad end without breakthrough as regional tensions continue
item.description: The article discusses the recent high-level US-Iran talks held in Islamabad, which concluded without any clear breakthrough but also without a complete breakdown, keeping hopes of continued diplomacy ...

item.title: Dar Gymkhana Club and MCL plant 600 trees in environmental conservation drive
item.description: Dar es Salaam Gymkhana Club has partnered with Mwananchi Communications Limited (MCL) to strengthen environmental conservation efforts through a tree planting exercise involving 600 trees within the c...

item.title: DBSA supports R2.5bn outcomes-based bond to fund water security and ecosystem restoration in South Africa
item.description: The Development Bank of Southern Africa (DBSA) has approved a R50m grant through its Green Fund to support the rollout of the Cape Water Outcomes-Based Bond, a financing initiative aimed at improving ...

item.title: Mark Carney’s Liberals secure majority, signalling a new phase in federal governance
item.description: The article argues that Prime Minister Mark Carney has effectively transitioned from leading a constrained minority government to a more stable majority, following a combination of byelection victorie...

item.title: Starmer proposes automatic adoption of EU rules with financial contributions
item.description: In his column for the Daily Mail, Daniel Hannan criticises Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer for proposing a policy that would see the United Kingdom automatically adopt future European Union rules in e...
